title: Saadoon-Kauldi
content:

{{Quote|When does a smuggler know he's good? The day you have a face-to-face meeting with Saadoon-Kauldi and leave with your mind and limbs intact.|[[Karl Mathieu Ancher]], friend of Saadoon-Kauldi|<ref name="Black Sands" />}}
'''Saadoon-Kauldi''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Kadri'Ra]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] who was freed by members of the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Badawzi family]]. Out of gratitude, Saadoon-Kauldi became an indentured servant to the Badawzis, and he eventually parted from them on good terms. He then became a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Drug dealer|dealing]] with [[Spice/Legends|spice]] and formed his [[Saadoon-Kauldi's criminal empire|own criminal empire]]. During his career as a criminal, Saadoon-Kauldi used his resources to [[Saadoon-Kauldi's anti-slaver campaign|free as many slaves]] as he could. Due to this, many former slaves became willing followers of Saadoon-Kauldi, increasing his power and influence. However, many [[slaver guild]]s opposed him and lobbied to put a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. After several [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempts, Saadoon-Kauldi grew increasingly paranoid. Using the [[Caelli-Merced Syndicate]] as a front, [[Abdi-Badawzi]], a Twi'lek crime lord operating in the [[Socorro system/Legends|Socorro system]], offered Saadoon-Kauldi the [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruiser]] ''[[Merkel]]'' in hopes of convincing the Kadri'Ra to move to [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] and become his business partner. Saadoon-Kauldi accepted and moved into the ''Merkel'' in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]]. He rarely left the [[Starship/Legends|ship]], even though he was surrounded by allies such as the [[Black Bha'lir]] and the [[Socorran Navy]].

Settled over Neftali, Saadoon-Kauldi made [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] through front companies and deals with local criminals. He helped his associates in moments of need and always showed respect for them. He was in turn widely respected both inside and outside of the Socorro [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Saadoon-Kauldi also began providing weapons to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] sponsored the [[Speciecide|decimation]] of the Kadri'Ra on [[Arapia]]. As the Empire was trying discredit the [[Kadri'Ra language]], Saadoon-Kauldi refused to speak in any other [[Language/Legends|language]]. He also tried to recruit the young [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Drake Paulsen]], but Paulsen decided to join the Black Bha'lir instead. Later, Paulsen and his allies, including the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Thaddeus Ross]] and [[Tait Ransom]], became wanted by the Empire for the [[Death/Legends|murder]] of the Twi'lek [[Izzat]]. Because they were members of one of Saadoon-Kauldi's allies, the Black Bha'lir, the Kadri'Ra offered to lodge and protect Ross and Ransom.

==Biography==
===Origins===
Saadoon-Kauldi, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kadri'Ra]], was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Arapia]],<ref name="Black Sands" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the immigrated, [[dragon]]-like Kadri'Ra [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> Born a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Saadoon-Kauldi never learned to manipulate or sense [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="force">Per [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']], only characters trained in the Force do have Force skills, and those are listed in the character's stats. Kauldi is mentioned to be Force-sensitive in ''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'', but he has no Force skills.</ref> At some point, Saadoon-Kauldi was [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]], although he was later freed by the [[Badawzi family]],<ref name="Black Sands" /> a powerful and influential clan of [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] from the planet [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]].<ref name="Black Sands" /> Having nothing of value to offer his saviors, Saadoon-Kauldi considered himself indentured to the Badawzis. The Kadri'Ra served the Badawzis for [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and the Twi'leks showed him a respect that he had not known as a slave. Eventually, Saadoon-Kauldi left the Badawzis on good terms and began his own business—a [[Saadoon-Kauldi's criminal empire|criminal empire]].<ref name="Black Sands" />

===Creating his own business===
Saadoon-Kauldi managed to grasp control over certain pre-existing [[Spice/Legends|spice]] runs. He then hired [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and commandeered spice processing facilities and [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowports]]. Through [[Drug dealer|drug dealing]], he became a respected and promising [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. Saadoon-Kauldi based his power in forming alliances with other criminals rather than using fear to motivate his subjects like most crime lords did. He continued to increase his empire and obtained assets in the [[Arapia system/Legends|Arapia system]]. The Kadri'Ra had at least five thousand direct agents working in [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]], laundering, smuggling—including drug trade and gunrunning—and usury.<ref name="Black Sands" />

At some point before [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]], while Saadoon-Kauldi was working with some smugglers, he boarded a slaver [[Galleon (Starship class)|galleon]]. When he saw the state of the slaves, he lost his temper and [[Massacre on the galleon|butchered]] the slavers, setting all the slaves free. Most of the freed slaves swore loyalty and service to Saadoon-Kauldi, increasing the influence of his empire and the area it covered. Saadoon-Kauldi decided to respect his newly indentured serfs in the same way that he had been respected by the Badawzis. Understanding that he could further expand his power by liberating more slaves, he decided to temporarily stop his smuggling operations and initiate an [[Saadoon-Kauldi's anti-slaver campaign|anti-slaver campaign]], dedicating all of his resources to the abduction of slaver [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. This maneuver allowed him to emancipate a great number of slaves, many of whom turned into followers of his empire.<ref name="Black Sands" />

During one of these operations, he [[Rescue at the Imperial garbage scow|attacked]] an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Garbage scow/Legends|garbage scow]], unshackling hundreds of slaves. One of those slaves, an elderly [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] named [[Guzald]], decided to serve and follow Saadoon-Kauldi very closely. One certain [[slaver guild]], unhappy with Saadoon-Kauldi's campaign, coerced its contacts, including Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officers]], to take measures against the Kadri'Ra's recent moves against slavery. They offered an official, substantial [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Saadoon-Kauldi's head, but he paid no attention to it. Once Saadoon-Kauldi believed that he had released enough slaves—the number of former slaves working for Saadoon-Kauldi was greater than the ranks of any of Saadoon-Kauldi's own allies—the crime lord returned to drug smuggling.<ref name="Black Sands" />

===Looking for safer headquarters===
Saadoon-Kauldi became a prominent crime lord, and from that point on, he avoided being seen in public. After several [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempts against him, including at least one on Arapia, he grew increasingly paranoid. The Empire also began to actively attempt to capture Saadoon-Kauldi and enslave him. In the meantime, as a Kadri'Ra, Saadoon-Kauldi continued to grow larger for as long as he lived—he had already reached seventy-five [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length—making it increasingly more difficult for him to remain hidden.<ref name="Black Sands" /> A Twi'lek crime lord by the name of [[Abdi-Badawzi]], whose great-grandfather was rumored to have been released at the same time as Saadoon-Kauldi and whose family had been served by Saadoon-Kauldi previously, began asking the Kadri'Ra for advice in the Twi'lek's venture to expand criminal influence outside [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. Abdi-Badawzi added Saadoon-Kauldi to his contact network, although they did not officially became partners or full-fledged allies.<ref name="Black Sands" />

The Twi'lek then discovered that Saadoon-Kauldi was worried about the Empire's attempts against the Kadri'Ra's freedom and decided to take measures to protect the Kadri'Ra, hoping to gain him as a friend. Abdi-Badawzi wanted an important, nearby ally supporting him, which would allow him to obtain greater influence and expand the borders of his [[Abdi-Badawzi's organization|criminal empire]] to areas where Saadoon-Kauldi could protect him.<ref name="Black Sands" /> Hoping to get Saadoon-Kauldi to move close to his headquarters, Badawzi sent [[Pret Swain]], the manager of the Socorro-based [[Caelli-Merced Syndicate]], to Saadoon-Kauldi to sell the Kadri'Ra a very cheap [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruiser]] for use as a mobile, secure habitat. The ship, ''[[Merkel]]'', was a customized [[Action V transport]] from [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]]—it had a [[black]] helm for stealth and hidden weaponry. As soon as Saadoon-Kauldi bought it, the ship was adapted to the Kadri'Ra's specific needs. Saadoon-Kauldi then allied himself with Abdi-Badawzi, allowing the Kadri'Ra to settle in the [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the planet [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]], in the [[Socorro system/Legends|Socorro system]].<ref name="Black Sands" />

===Life in the Socorro system===
As soon as he settled down over Neftali, Saadoon-Kauldi made contact with another local criminal group, the [[Black Bha'lir]],<ref name="Black Sands" /> a syndicate offering "coverage" to members in trouble.<ref name="Black Sands" /> As the Bha'lir was opposed to slavery, Saadoon-Kauldi soon allied himself with them. The Socorran [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], famous for their integrity, also allied themselves with Saadoon-Kauldi. The Kadri'Ra developed many other contacts, including the Socorran government, whose [[Socorran Navy|Navy]] was prepared to attack any unauthorized ships approaching the ''Merkel''. Saadoon-Kauldi, increasingly worried by the frequent attempts on his life, rarely left Neftali's orbit, preferring to stay within the protection of the Socorran [[Navy/Legends|Navy]].<ref name="Black Sands" />

[[File:Saadonandfriends.jpg|left|thumb|Saadoon-Kauldi holds a meeting using Guzald as an interpreter.]]
As Saadoon-Kauldi became a public figure in the Socorran [[Star system/Legends|system]], several rumors concerning him appeared—which was usual with well-known citizens of the system. Locals said that Saadoon-Kauldi had been kidnapped from Arapia and transported as a slave, a trait that made him sympathetic to Socorran [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="Black Sands" /> Abdi-Badawzi then offered Saadoon-Kauldi<ref name="Black Sands" /> an interest of one-third<ref name="Black Sands" /> of [[Ethra Brewery]]—a lucrative business that distributed Socorran [[raava]]—because the Twi'lek wanted a business partner from outside Socorro to improve the extra-system contacts of Ethra.<ref name="Black Sands" /> Meanwhile, Saadoon-Kauldi continued his efforts to expand his organization. Caelli-Merced Syndicate agreed to pay him money for each of their exports. Other criminal activities of his empire included credit laundering; gambling; illegal drug dealing; [[Loan shark|loansharking]]; and smuggling, including arms trafficking. Saadoon-Kauldi earned more money, however, by acting as a mediator among criminal groups, stopping gang wars, and other similar situations of unrest.<ref name="Black Sands" />

Circa 10 BBY, the smuggler [[Saylor Marjan|Saylor "Marji" Marjan]] owed money to Saadoon-Kauldi, so the Kadri'Ra sent two agents, [[Toob Ancher]] and [[Fahs Oxsor]], to Arapia to collect the money. As Ancher and Marjan had been friends, Marjan convinced Ancher to smuggle a sizable consignment of spice to the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]], a run that would allow Marjan to earn enough money to pay the debt and get a profit. Ancher did so, but the supposed profit was not enough to cover Ancher's expenses, as his ship was damaged during the run. Saadoon-Kauldi did not [[Death/Legends|kill]] Marjan for this, but Ancher did not forgive Marjan either.<ref name="ABW">{{JournalCite|5|A Bitter Winter}}</ref>

===Frictions with the Galactic Empire===
Around this time, Saadoon-Kauldi discovered that the Galactic Empire was directly responsible for several activities against the Kadri'Ra species, including discrediting the [[Kadri'Ra language]] and sponsoring a [[Speciecide|holocaust]] that dwindled the number of Kadri'Ra on Arapia. Saddened by the hardships that his species was facing, Saadoon-Kauldi became a supporter of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]<ref name="Black Sands" /> at some point after the [[Corellian Treaty|creation]] of the Alliance in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].<ref name="reb">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref> He regularly sent weapons to the Alliance, using Caelli-Merced as his middleman. Growing larger and more restless, Saadoon-Kauldi decided around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] that he wanted a more permanent residence. As he continued to grow, he eventually became too large to fit in the ''Merkel''.<ref name="Black Sands" />

In approximately [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="Black Sands" /> [[Kaine Paulsen]], [[Parent/Legends|father]] and mentor to the pirate [[Drake Paulsen]], was murdered. Saadoon-Kauldi offered young Paulsen a job, but several other underworld factions did the same, including Abdi-Badawzi's organization, [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure's criminal empire]], and [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. Paulsen initially rejected all of them, but finally joined the Black Bha'lir.<ref name="Black Sands" /> Paulsen and his allies—including the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Nikaede Celso]] and the smugglers [[Thaddeus Ross]] and [[Tait Ransom]]—then assassinated [[Izzat]], a Twi'lek who had been involved in the murder of Paulsen's father. Izzat had been working for the Empire at that time, and the Empire posted very large bounties for the four criminals. The Black Bha'lir hurried to help these beings, as they were members of the organization. As an ally of the Black Bha'lir, Saadoon-Kauldi offered to lodge Ross and Ransom for a time.<ref name="Black Sands" />

==Personality and traits==
Saadoon-Kauldi was intelligent, shrewd, and cultured, and he used all of these traits when creating his criminal empire. He had low [[Technician/Legends|technical]] abilities and was a sub-par [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].<ref name="Black Sands" /> He was also an untrained Force-sensitive.<ref name="force" /> Many [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smugglers living on Socorro wanted to work for him, because they had heard that Saadoon-Kauldi rewarded efficient employees with money and favors, and forgave employees who failed him once. He made several alliances that sustained his power and influence. Unlike other crime lords, the Kadri'Ra showed respect for the people he dealt with, even when he had no need to do so. Saadoon-Kauldi was respected among criminals, and sometimes admired as if he were a [[King/Legends|king]]. The [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], who were as long-lived as the Kadri'Ra, had a good opinion of Saadoon-Kauldi. Saadoon-Kauldi had no enemies among criminals, except for slavers, and was well regarded even by certain [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agencies]].<ref name="Black Sands" />

Although Saadoon-Kauldi was able to speak many [[Language/Legends|languages]], he refused to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]. In protest at the Galactic Empire's attempts to eradicate the Kadri'Ra culture and language, Saadoon-Kauldi used only that tongue. As this was a difficult, rarely learned tongue, Saadoon-Kauldi commonly resorted to a translator, his servant Guzald. Any person wanting an audience with Saadoon-Kauldi was free to bring his or her own translator of Kadri'Ra.<ref name="Black Sands" />

==Behind the scenes==
Although Saadoon-Kauldi was described in great detail in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying-game]] supplement ''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'', he never appeared in any narrative source. He was only mentioned in passing by a character of the tale ''[[A Bitter Winter]]''.

==Appearances==
*{{JournalCite|5|A Bitter Winter|reprint=1}} {{1stm}}
